task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2199: feat(storyboard): Add template selection UI to Generate step ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2199` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2199 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat(storyboard): Add template selection UI to Generate step (#2199)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2199` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2199" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat(storyboard): Add template selection UI to Generate step" body: "Closes #2199\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Create minimal template definitions (type + 5 templates) as a self-contained config since prior steps haven't delivered these - Build template picker using RadioGroup card pattern from existing PlanPicker component, wrapped in a dialog for action bar integration - Persist selection to existing `presentations.template_id` database field via new server action - Pass template ID through export flow and apply basic color theming to PPTX output, deferring full generator refactoring to Step 2
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Template definitions do not exist from prior steps **Options Considered:** 1. Block this ticket until Step 3 is completed 2. Create minimal template definitions inline as part of this ticket **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Create minimal template definitions as a self-contained config file, allowing the UI work to proceed while keeping template data easily updatable when Step 3 formalizes the structure.
Design Choice 2: TemplateConfig generator integration scope **Options Considered:** 1. Fully refactor pptx-generator to use TemplateConfig theming 2. Pass template ID to export action and apply basic theme properties where feasible **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Pass template ID through the export flow and apply the template's primary colors/fonts to the existing generator. Full generator refactoring belongs to Step 2 (`#2197`), not this UI ticket.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Template Definitions and Types Establish the template data model and create the 5 curated template definitions that the picker will display.
Task 1: Create Template Type Definitions Define the TypeScript interface and Zod schema for presentation templates, establishing the contract for template metadata. - Create new schema file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-template.schema.ts` - Define `PresentationTemplate` interface with fields: `id`, `name`, `description`, `colors` (array of hex strings), `thumbnailUrl`, and optional `fonts` configuration - Create corresponding Zod schema for runtime validation - Export a `TemplateId` union type for the 5 template IDs
Task 2: Define the 5 Curated Templates Create the template registry containing all template metadata for UI display and export configuration. - Create config file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/config/presentation-templates.config.ts` - Define 5 templates following the pattern: Consulting Classic, Modern Minimal, Creative Bold, Corporate Professional, and Tech Forward (or similar names aligned with design direction) - Each template includes: unique ID, display name, 1-line description, 5-6 color hex codes for palette preview, placeholder thumbnail path - Export a `getTemplateById()` utility function and `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_ID` constant - Use placeholder thumbnail paths pointing to `public/images/templates/` (actual images can be added later)
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Establish the template data model and create 5 curated template definitions. The goal is to provide the foundational types and data that the template picker UI will consume. **Create Template Type Definitions:** - Create schema file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-template.schema.ts` - Define `PresentationTemplate` interface with these fields: - `id`: string identifier - `name`: display name - `description`: 1-line description - `colors`: array of hex color strings - `thumbnailUrl`: string path to preview image - `fonts` (optional): font configuration object - Create corresponding Zod schema for runtime validation - Export a `TemplateId` union type containing the 5 template IDs **Create Template Registry:** - Create config file at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/config/presentation-templates.config.ts` - Define 5 template objects with names like: Consulting Classic, Modern Minimal, Creative Bold, Corporate Professional, and Tech Forward - Each template must include: - Unique ID - Display name - 1-line description suitable for UI display - 5-6 hex color codes representing the palette - Placeholder thumbnail path pointing to `public/images/templates/` - Export `getTemplateById()` utility function that accepts a template ID and returns the template object - Export `DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_ID` constant pointing to one of the 5 templates ```
### Phase 2: Template Picker Component Build the template selection UI component following existing design patterns.
Task 1: Create Color Swatch Preview Component Build a small reusable component to display a template's color palette as visual swatches. - Create component at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/storyboard/_components/template-color-swatches.tsx` - Accept `colors` array prop and render as small circular or rounded swatches in a horizontal row - Apply appropriate sizing (12-16px diameter) and spacing for compact display within cards - Add `data-testid` attribute for testing
Task 2: Create Template Picker Component Build the main template picker using the RadioGroup card selection pattern from PlanPicker. - Create component at `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/storyboard/_components/template-picker.tsx` - Use `RadioGroup` with `RadioGroupItemLabel` wrapper from `packages/ui/src/shadcn/radio-group.tsx` - Render responsive grid layout: `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` with appropriate gap - Each card displays: template name, description text, `TemplateColorS
label: neo-issue-2199
